steps.title: Compare the two ships
steps.content: Both the Dreadnought and the Royal George had three decks. But only the Royal George was ranked first-rate, so having three decks was not enough by itself.

steps.title: Draw only the proven claim
steps.content: Choice A wrongly gives the non-first-rate Dreadnought a first-rate crew range. Choice B is contradicted by the Dreadnought. Choice C invents a crew need beyond 875. The supported conclusion is that three decks did not always mean first-rate status. The answer is D.
